market: Global markets in late March 2026 faced downward pressure, with the S&P 500 down roughly 7.5% year-to-date by March 30, marking its worst month since 2022. Market weakness was driven by mega-cap tech underperformance and concerns over inflation and war-driven oil prices. Energy stocks performed strongly, soaring ~40% YTD. Intel (INTC) shares fell ~5% on March 30 amid broader sector volatility, despite long-term optimism around the 18A process node. Amazon (AMZN) declined 5.1% in the final week of March, with investors scrutinizing high AI capex spending.
art: March 2026 was a landmark month. TEFAF Maastricht (March 14-19) saw over 50,000 visitors and robust sales, confirming its status as a premier global art event. Concurrently, the 'Renoir and Love' exhibition opened at the Musée d'Orsay on March 17, drawing significant attention as the first major Renoir retrospective in France since 1985, featuring over 50 paintings and running through July 2026.
